# python: support venv also as virtenv

**Motivation:** a lot of examples contain `python3 -m venv .venv`, e.g., https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/python/environments#_create-a-virtual-environment-in-the-terminal

Fix https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Akamilsk%2Fdotfiles%20virtenv&type=code.
